steps.action: Pressed the native `Claude Code 1` sidebar button, then activated the `View skill Frontend Design` control from the rendered platform card.
steps.expected: Clicking a platform skill should navigate into that skill's detail page.
steps.observed: The native Tauri window URL changed to `/skill/frontend-design` and the detail view rendered the Frontend Design header plus metadata sections, confirming platform-card navigation into the detail route.

steps.action: Inspected the skill detail header and metadata section after opening Frontend Design in the native app.
steps.expected: The detail page should show name, description, file path, source, and an installation timestamp.
steps.observed: The page displayed the Frontend Design name/description, file path, canonical path, source, scanned-at metadata, and per-platform installed-at timestamps in the installation list, satisfying the required metadata surface.

steps.action: Reviewed the Installation Status section for Frontend Design across the available platforms.
steps.expected: Each platform should show whether the skill is installed, along with the appropriate install or uninstall action.
steps.observed: The detail page listed every non-central platform with either an `Install` button or an installed row with `Uninstall`, installed path text, and installed-at timestamp, matching the underlying fixture state.

steps.action: Resized the native window for full content visibility and switched to the Markdown preview tab.
steps.expected: The SKILL.md content should render as formatted Markdown with headings, lists, and code blocks.
steps.observed: The preview rendered formatted headings, bullet lists, and fenced code styling rather than raw source text.

steps.action: Activated the `Raw Source` tab via native accessibility and dumped the AX tree for the content region.
steps.expected: The raw tab should show unrendered SKILL.md text including YAML frontmatter.
steps.observed: The raw content region exposed the literal `---` frontmatter block plus markdown source text in the AX tree, confirming the tab switched away from rendered Markdown to raw source.

steps.action: Clicked `Install to Cursor` from the Frontend Design detail page, then verified the filesystem and SQLite fixture state.
steps.expected: Installing from detail should create the platform link and update the status in the UI.
steps.observed: The page updated to show Cursor as installed, the fixture filesystem contained the new `.cursor/skills/frontend-design` symlink, and SQLite recorded the installation row for Cursor.

evidence.consoleErrors: The detail install completed without surfacing any user-visible command error.

steps.action: Clicked `Uninstall from Cursor` from the same detail page and rechecked the fixture filesystem and SQLite database.
steps.expected: Uninstalling from detail should remove the platform link and return the UI to the uninstalled state.
steps.observed: The page returned Cursor to an `Install` action, the `.cursor/skills/frontend-design` symlink was removed from disk, and the matching SQLite installation row no longer existed.

evidence.consoleErrors: The detail uninstall completed without surfacing any user-visible command error.

steps.action: Inspected the detail-page header controls after arriving from the platform view.
steps.expected: A back-arrow control should be available so the user can return to the prior view.
steps.observed: The detail header exposed a `Go back` button in the native accessibility tree, confirming the back-navigation affordance is present on the real user surface.

steps.action: Started from the Claude Code platform view, navigated into Frontend Design detail, installed the skill to Cursor, and compared the resulting UI with filesystem/database state.
steps.expected: The install journey should keep the detail flow consistent and persist a real installation that other views can consume.
steps.observed: The platform-originated detail page successfully installed to Cursor, immediately reflected Cursor as installed in the detail status list, and persisted the new symlink/SQLite row for the shared scan data.

evidence.consoleErrors: The full install journey completed without user-visible errors.

steps.action: Uninstalled the same Cursor link from the detail page after a successful install and compared the post-action UI with the underlying fixture state.
steps.expected: Removing the installation should clear the shared install record so the user-facing surfaces return to the unlinked state.
steps.observed: The detail status immediately reverted Cursor to `Install`, the symlink disappeared from the platform directory, and the installation row was removed from SQLite, leaving no stale linked state for downstream views.

evidence.consoleErrors: The uninstall journey completed without user-visible errors.

frictions.description: Nested `user-testing-flow-validator` delegation was not permitted in this worker session.
frictions.resolution: Completed the assigned assertions manually inside the worker using native macOS accessibility automation and direct evidence capture.

frictions.description: The browser preview did not expose Tauri globals, so the web surface could not exercise real install/uninstall behavior.
frictions.resolution: Validated the native `skills-manage` window itself and captured evidence with macOS screencapture plus AX tree dumps.

frictions.description: Quartz window-image capture produced skewed screenshots for this app window.
frictions.resolution: Switched to native `screencapture` for the evidence images while keeping the PyObjC helper for navigation and AX inspection.

summary: Validated all 10 skill-detail assertions against the native Tauri app running on port 24200 with an isolated HOME fixture. Platform-to-detail navigation, metadata/install status, markdown/raw tabs, detail-page install/uninstall actions, and the cross-flow install/uninstall journeys all passed with screenshot, AX-tree, filesystem, and SQLite evidence.
